concerns.text: FR-006 mandates 5 seeds for No-Score/Score-Default and 3 seeds for each of the 12 ablation variants (total 46 runs). The plan (Phase 3, Risk Mitigation) explicitly reduces this to 3 seeds for baseline/default and 1 seed for ablation (18 runs total) due to runtime constraints. This is a silent under-planning of the spec's statistical power requirement.

FR-003 requires executing a training instance for *each* combination of the hyperparameter grid (12 variants). The plan (Phase 3) reduces this to '1 seed for each grid point' (12 runs) but the total run count reduction in FR-006 implies the full grid might not be executed as specified, or the seed count per grid point is insufficient for the statistical comparison required by FR-007.

The spec assumes a '4-core CPU runner with 16GB RAM' for SC-005 and Assumptions. The plan targets '2-core/7GB RAM' (GitHub Actions free-tier). The plan does not explicitly address the discrepancy in hardware resources required to meet the spec's feasibility criterion (SC-005) or the 24-hour budget.

FR-004 requires recording steps to cross '[deferred] of the maximum pilot score'. The plan (Research.md) defines the threshold as '80% of the best pilot score' but the spec text uses '[deferred]' as a placeholder for the percentage. The plan does not explicitly confirm the 80% value is the intended 'deferred' value to be used, creating ambiguity in the measurement definition.

FR-006 requires 3 seeds for *each* Score-Ablation variant. The plan (Phase 3) states 'Run 1 seed for each grid point'. This reduces the statistical power for the ablation variants from 3 to 1, violating the seed count requirement for the ablation group.
